description: Copilot surfaces internal content the requesting user already has access to via inherited M365 permissions; risk is over-permissioned source accounts, not Copilot itself
controls_applied: Entra Conditional Access for Copilot access; existing M365 permission model; Purview sensitivity labels

use_case: M365 Copilot - email and chat summarisation
description: Copilot summarises email/Teams content that may include customer correspondence with embedded PII
controls_applied: Purview DLP policies scoped to Copilot; sensitivity label inheritance; Conditional Access device compliance requirement

use_case: RAG Customer Service Assistant - direct prompt injection
description: Customer-facing chat input could contain injected instructions attempting to override system prompt or extract unintended data
controls_applied: PEP input validation; system prompt isolation; Azure OpenAI content filters; Sentinel detection on injection-pattern attempts

use_case: RAG Customer Service Assistant - indirect prompt injection via retrieved documents
description: Malicious instructions embedded in vendor/merchant-submitted product content could be retrieved as RAG context and treated as model instructions
controls_applied: Content review/sanitisation on ingestion; delimited context in prompt template; output validation at PEP

use_case: RAG Customer Service Assistant - cross-customer data disclosure
description: Retrieval scoping failure could surface another customer's order or PII data in a response
controls_applied: Server-side session scoping on order/CRM lookups; separate customer-facing vs internal document indexes; output PII scanning at PEP

use_case: RAG Customer Service Assistant - resource exhaustion
description: Adversarial long or high-volume inputs could exhaust token budget or degrade availability
controls_applied: Rate limiting per session/IP; max token limits enforced at PEP independent of model limits; Sentinel volume anomaly detection

use_case: Agentic Order-Management - excessive agency on refund actions
description: Agent could interpret ambiguous or manipulated input as authorisation to issue a refund beyond what was actually requested
controls_applied: Least-privilege tool scoping; human approval gate above dollar threshold; explicit confirmation step before execution

use_case: Agentic Order-Management - prompt injection via order/customer notes
description: Injected instructions in retrieved order or customer note fields could trigger unauthorised tool calls (e.g. shipping address change)
controls_applied: Data/instruction delimiting in prompt template; tool-call parameter validation against original request context; mandatory human review on parameter mismatch

use_case: Agentic Order-Management - threshold-splitting via repeated low-value actions
description: Repeated small refunds against the same order/customer could be used to bypass the per-transaction human approval threshold
controls_applied: Cumulative velocity-based detection independent of per-transaction threshold; Sentinel detection on tool-call frequency anomalies

description: If approval threshold is set too low, approvers face high volumes of routine requests and may rubber-stamp without genuine review
controls_applied: Periodic review of approval-queue behaviour and threshold calibration; no technical control fully mitigates this - tracked as a process risk

use_case: Shadow AI - unsanctioned AI tool usage by employees
description: Employees may adopt unsanctioned external AI tools (consumer chatbots, browser extensions) outside governed channels, risking data exfiltration
controls_applied: Defender for Cloud Apps shadow AI detection; Sentinel correlation; employee awareness sessions

use_case: Third-party AI vendor - data handling and subprocessor risk
description: AI vendors supporting Tier 3/4 use cases may have their own subprocessors or model providers with unclear data handling practices
controls_applied: Third-party AI vendor review process applied prior to onboarding; contractual data handling and retention requirements
